content:
https://rust.docs.kernel.org/kernel/list/struct.List.html#examples

Not sure if I fully understand. Once I have a struct that contains ListLinks, I'll always have to create the struct being contained in a ListArc?
You need ListArc to insert to the linked list, yes.

Can I wrap it into a ListArc later?

I want to give a struct to users and then later enqueue it into my list in my API, once the user passes it. I don't want them to carry around the ListArc container
You can have them carry around Arc or UniqueArc instead. I don't think you'll get around a container.

But I could have something like that

struct Job {…};

struct EnqueuedJob {
   List<Job>,
}

Jobqueue::submit_job(job: Job);

couldn't I.

And consuming a struct by value should make it impossible for the user to wrap it into a container extending the lifetime, such as an Arc, right?

You can put the ListArc inside Job.

struct Job(ListArc<JobInner>);
btw, is the intended usecase for List always interaction with C? Because we also have KVec. When would one use which?
